title: NOC 72411 – Auto body collision, refinishing and glass technicians and damage repair estimators | GTR Immigration
description: In 2026, Auto Body and Collision Technicians (NOC 72411) are the restoration masters of Canada’s automotive sector. As vehicles become increasingly complex with Advanced Driver Assistance Systems (ADAS) and lightweight composite frames, these professionals have evolved into high-tech repair specialists. Beyond traditional metalwork and painting, they now calibrate the sensors and cameras integrated into body panels and windshields. Operating at the TEER 2 level, they are essential to Canada’s multi-billion dollar collision repair industry. In 2026, the sector is seeing strong demand as a wave of retirements coincides with an increase in vehicle sophisticated repairs.

classification.teer: FeatureDetailsBroad Occupational Category7 – Trades, transport and equipment operators and related occupationsTEER Category2 (Occupations usually requiring college, apprenticeship, or supervisory experience)Major Group72 – Technical trades and transportation officers and controllersSub-Major Group724 – Machine repair and maintenance trades

duties: Damage Assessment: Reviewing damage reports and cost estimates, and planning work for structural or aesthetic restoration.
Structural Repair: Straightening bent frames using computerized pulling and anchoring equipment; repairing or replacing front-end and underbody components.
Surface Refinishing: Hammering out dents, filling buckles with plastic fillers, and grinding/sanding surfaces using high-power tools.
Precision Painting: Mixing paint, blending colors, and repainting surfaces using advanced spray guns in climate-controlled booths.
Estimating (Estimators): Inspecting vehicles to estimate repair costs and times, coordinating with insurance companies, and managing work processes.
Glass & Interior: Repairing and replacing windshields, sunroofs, and interior components like seat frames and floorboard insulation.
employment_requirements: Education: Completion of secondary school is typically required.
Apprenticeship: Completion of a three- to four-year apprenticeship program OR a combination of over four years of work experience and industry courses is required for certification.

Certification: Trade certification is compulsory in Ontario and Alberta (for auto body and collision repairers). It is voluntary but highly recommended in all other provinces for higher wages and union roles.
Specific Licenses: In Ontario, certification from Skilled Trades Ontario is mandatory for "Auto Body and Collision Damage Repairer."
Red Seal: The Interprovincial Standards Red Seal is the "Gold Standard," allowing for mobility across Canada and access to high-paying national insurance-preferred shops.
labour_market_demand: The 2026 outlook is Moderate to Good, with "High Hiring Difficulty" reported by 84% of employers in several provinces:

Ontario: Very Good prospects. In February and March 2026, Ontario issued targeted OINP invitations for NOC 72411 under the Employer Job Offer: Foreign Worker and Skilled Trades streams. Minimum scores for regional draws in Southwestern and Northern Ontario started as low as 53–54, reflecting a critical need for body technicians in logistics and transit hubs.

Alberta: Strongest Demand. Employers in Alberta report significant hiring difficulties, with 34% of vacancies remaining unfilled for over 4 months. Median wages are competitive at $31.04/hr, with top earners reaching $52.88/hr.

British Columbia: Good prospects. BC projects a high median wage of $31.82/hr, with top-tier earners in the "Mainland/Southwest" region reaching $47.65/hr. The province is actively recruiting for technicians skilled in glass replacement and calibration.
National Trend: Approximately 78% of workers in this NOC receive comprehensive non-wage benefits. The 2026 market highly favors those who can perform ADAS calibration alongside structural repairs.
Why Work in Canada as an Auto Body Technician?
Lead the Restoration Tech: Gain experience with 2026-grade technologies, including digital frame-pulling systems, spectrophotometers for perfect paint matching, and ultrasonic sensor testing.
Unbeatable Job Stability: Your skills are vital for the insurance and logistics sectors. 75% of collision technicians work all year round—higher than most construction trades.
High-Tier Compensation: As a TEER 2 trade, your median annual salary is approximately $69,364, with experienced estimators and master refinishers in major cities earning over $110,000.
Priority for PR: In 2026, this NOC is a core target for the Express Entry "Trade Occupations" category and is a priority for the OINP REDI Pilot draws.
pathways: OINP Employer Job Offer: In March 2026, OINP issued invitations to candidates in NOC 72411 with a score of 53+ (Southwestern Ontario). This is a prime route for those with an Ontario job offer.

2026 Express Entry (Trade Occupations Category): Updated for 2026. Eligible candidates now require 12 months of full-time work experience (increased from 6 months) within the last 3 years to receive an ITA at lower CRS thresholds.
2026 TR to PR Pathway: A confirmed initiative targeting up to 33,000 temporary residents. Collision technicians are classified as "High-Priority Infrastructure and Service Workers."
BC PNP Construction/Skilled Worker: A reliable route for technicians with a job offer from a BC-based body shop or appraisal center.
